commit.message:
fix(ci): suppress false-positive OWASP CVEs for keycloak-oauth2-starter

OWASP incorrectly matches the internal keycloak-oauth2-starter adapter JAR
against Keycloak server CVEs (CVE-2014-3651, CVE-2014-3709, CVE-2017-12161)
via CPE cpe:2.3:a:keycloak:keycloak:1.0. This is a false positive — the
adapter is not the Keycloak server product.

Add owasp-suppressions.xml and reference it via maven.multiModuleProjectDirectory
so it resolves correctly in all reactor modules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(test): use deterministic UUID in setupStandardMocks to prevent CPR pattern flakiness

UUID.randomUUID() could generate a UUID where a 6-digit decimal run in segment 1
spans the hyphen into a 4-digit segment 2, matching the CPR pattern \d{6}-\d{4}.
For example: 0b821041-2405... contains '821041-2405'.

Fix: replace with a fixed all-hex-letter UUID (cccccccc-cccc-4ccc-8ccc-cccccccccccc)
that structurally cannot match any CPR or 10-digit pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): use fully-qualified Maven coordinates for OWASP plugin

Maven cannot resolve 'dependency-check:' prefix because org.owasp is not
in the default plugin groups (org.apache.maven.plugins, org.codehaus.mojo).
Use full groupId:artifactId:goal syntax to invoke the plugin directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(catala): disambiguate exception hierarchy with label a2_base

In scope AfbrydelseValidering, afbrydelseResultat had two unlabeled base
definitions (line 299: BEROSTILLELSE+PSRM, line 331: LOENINDEHOLDELSE_VARSEL),
causing 'Ambiguous exception' errors for all 4 exceptions.

Fix: add 'label a2_base' to the line-331 base definition (A-2), then
change all 4 exception blocks to 'exception a2_base' explicitly.

Exceptions A-2b (LOENINDEHOLDELSE_AFGOERELSE+!registered),
A-3 (LOENINDEHOLDELSE_AFGOERELSE+registered),
A-4 (LOENINDEHOLDELSE_AFGOERELSE+harInaktivSiden),
A-5a (UDLAEG+ORDINARY) now unambiguously override A-2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): fix duration syntax — use 'N day' not 'duration of N days'

In Catala 1.1.0, duration literals use singular unit keywords:
  365 day (not 365 days, not duration of 365 days)
  3 * 365 day = integer * duration = 1095-day duration

'duration of' is not a Catala construct — it was treated as a variable
reference 'duration' followed by the 'of' operator, causing parse errors.

Changes in ga_2_4_foraeldelse.catala_da:
- Remove 'duration of ()' construct; keep inner arithmetic (9 occurrences)
- Change 'days' -> 'day' throughout
- Add missing 'consequence' before standalone 'equals' at line 605

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): remove (* *) inline comments invalid in Catala 1.1.0 parser

Remove 65 inline (* ... *) comments from scope context declarations in
ga_2_3_2_1_daekningsraekkefoeigen.catala_da and ga_2_4_foraeldelse.catala_da.

Catala 1.1.0 parser does not accept (* *) at end of context declarations;
valid tokens after a type name are 'input', 'output', 'internal', 'state', etc.
The comments were explanatory only and do not affect logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): correct Catala 1.1.0 definition/rule syntax

- Conditional non-boolean: rule → definition, keep 'consequence equals V'
  (definition X under condition C consequence equals V)
- Boolean: rule stays, consequence equals true/false →
  consequence fulfilled/not fulfilled
- Unconditional non-boolean (no under condition): collapse to single-line
  'definition X equals V' without consequence keyword

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: don't bump chat to top of sidebar on open

MarkChatChanged no longer touches UpdatedAt — it only marks the chat
dirty for persistence. The timestamp bump now lives in QueueSaveChat
and SaveCurrentChatAsync where touchIndex: true already identifies
real user/assistant activity.

title generation, agent/skill/project changes) go through the same
MarkChatChanged but never set touchIndex, so UpdatedAt stays put and
the sidebar order is preserved.

for side-by-side comparison with diff tools like Beyond Compare.

Features:
- Independent source/target connection parameters
- Per-table or shared WHERE clauses
- Auto-detected primary key ordering
- Exclude generated/serial/identity columns
- Secure password prompting
- Parallel export with progress reporting

commit.message:
Fix ghost chat accumulation in sidebar

Clean up orphaned chats on startup — chats whose message file is
missing or empty on disk are removed from the index. These ghost
entries accumulated when the index was saved but the per-chat
message file was never written (e.g. due to a save race or the
process being killed between the two writes).

Additionally, guard the independent index save in title generation
so it only persists when the chat's message file already exists,
preventing the race condition that created most ghost entries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

boolean, etc.). Add --language en to each catala typecheck invocation.

BFF portals). Change to @ConditionalOnBean(DataSource.class) so the audit beans
are only registered when an actual DataSource bean exists in the context. This

commit.message:
Fix CLI reconnect cascade that kills all active sessions

Root cause: when the CLI process exited, every subscribed session
independently called ForceReconnectAsync, each creating a new CLI
process and killing the previous one — a cascade that destabilized
all sessions.

Changes:

1. Move auto-reconnect into CopilotService — SubscribeToCliProcessExit
   now calls ForceReconnectAsync internally before firing CliProcessExited.
   Per-session handlers only clean up UI state and show error messages;
   they no longer trigger reconnection.

2. Remove reconnect from SessionShutdownEvent handler — session shutdown
   is a per-session event (the CLI process is still alive serving other
   sessions). Calling ForceReconnectAsync here killed the entire CLI.

3. Guard ConnectCoreAsync against cascade — when forceReconnect is true,
   check if another caller already reconnected while waiting on the
   semaphore. Skip if the generation changed and client is healthy.

4. Call SetModelAsync after session resume — the SDK does not honor the
   model in ResumeSessionConfig; sessions stayed on the model they were
   created with. Now explicitly syncs to the user's current selection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Restore explicit title generation alongside SDK titles

The SDK's SessionTitleChangedEvent is not reliably emitted for all
session configurations (especially with appended system prompts).
Restore GenerateTitleAsync which creates a lightweight session with
the fastest model to generate titles immediately on new chats.

Both sources can update the title — our custom generation fires
first (fast model, dedicated prompt), and the SDK title can
override it later if it arrives. This provides the best UX:
immediate title on chat creation with SDK refinement as a bonus.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Simplify architecture by removing SDK workarounds

Remove thin CopilotService pass-through wrappers:
- SelectSessionAgentAsync → callers use session.Rpc.Agent.SelectAsync()
- DeselectSessionAgentAsync → callers use session.Rpc.Agent.DeselectAsync()
- SwitchSessionModelAsync → callers use session.SetModelAsync()
- ListSessionAgentsAsync → callers use session.Rpc.Agent.ListAsync()
- GetSessionEventsAsync → callers use session.GetMessagesAsync()

Use SDK-generated titles instead of separate sessions:
- Remove GenerateTitleAsync (created a throwaway session per chat)
- Remove GenerateTitleForChatAsync from ChatViewModel
- Rely on SessionTitleChangedEvent from the main session, which
  already updates the title in the event handler

Inline CreateLightweightSessionAsync:
- Convert to static BuildLightweightConfig() that returns a config
- Callers use CreateSessionAsync(BuildLightweightConfig(...)) directly
- Clearer intent, no hidden session creation

Deduplicate SessionConfigBuilder:
- Extract shared property population into Populate() helper
- Build() and BuildForResume() share the same logic for system
  message, skills, agents, tools, MCP, hooks, and agent selection
